input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Rewrite the problem so the next agent first checks whether the smallest candidate answer values are even possible, using modular arithmetic or direct contradiction before searching more broadly.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: Reformulated the prompt to enforce a feasibility check using modular arithmetic or parity constraints before proceeding to a full search/optimization strategy.

Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

results.content:
Reformulated the prompt to enforce a feasibility check using modular arithmetic or parity constraints before proceeding to a full search/optimization strategy.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Solve the following optimization problem: Given known polyomino oil-field shapes with unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with minimum probing cost using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

CRITICAL INSTRUCTION FOR INITIAL STEP: Before attempting to model the full search space or optimization strategy, perform a feasibility check on the smallest possible candidate answer values (e.g., minimum number of probes or queries). Use modular arithmetic, parity constraints, or direct contradiction to determine if these lower-bound values are mathematically possible given the constraints of the polyomino shapes and the query types. If the smallest candidate values are impossible, identify the contradiction and move to the next smallest feasible value. Only after establishing the mathematical feasibility of the cost bounds should you proceed to the broader search/optimization algorithm.
